tasks.title: Install PixiJS and create core canvas infrastructure
tasks.description:
Install PixiJS v8 and the particle emitter into the web app, then create the foundational canvas module at `apps/web/src/lib/canvas/`.

1. Run `pnpm --filter @twitch-hub/web add pixi.js@^8 @pixi/particle-emitter@^5`
2. Create `apps/web/src/lib/canvas/theme.ts` — export a color palette object (brand purple #7c3aed, silver #c0c0c0, steel #71717a, gold #fbbf24, diamond #22d3ee), easing curve functions (quartic sharp-out/in), and timing constants (BURST_DURATION=300, SWEEP_DURATION=600, SHOCKWAVE_DURATION=600).
3. Create `apps/web/src/lib/canvas/reduced-motion.ts` — detect `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` via matchMedia, export a `shouldAnimate()` function. All effects must check this.
4. Create `apps/web/src/lib/canvas/pixi-app.ts` — factory function `createPixiApp(canvas)` that returns a PixiJS `Application` configured with: transparent background, antialias on, resolution = devicePixelRatio, `powerPreference: 'low-power'` (OBS-friendly), auto-resize.
5. Create `apps/web/src/lib/canvas/PixiWrapper.svelte` — Svelte 5 component that: mounts a `<canvas>` element fixed behind HTML content (z-index 0, pointer-events: none), initializes the PixiJS app via the factory, exposes the app instance via `setContext('pixi-app')`, handles window resize and cleanup on destroy.
6. Create an `apps/web/src/lib/canvas/index.ts` barrel export.
acceptance_criteria: pixi.js and @pixi/particle-emitter appear in apps/web/package.json dependencies
theme.ts exports COLORS, EASING, and TIMING objects
reduced-motion.ts exports shouldAnimate() that returns false when prefers-reduced-motion is reduce
pixi-app.ts exports createPixiApp that returns a configured PixiJS Application
PixiWrapper.svelte mounts a canvas, initializes PixiJS, and sets context
pnpm build completes without errors
tasks.notes: Installed pixi.js@8.16.0 and @pixi/particle-emitter@5.0.10. Created canvas module at apps/web/src/lib/canvas/ with theme.ts (COLORS/EASING/TIMING), reduced-motion.ts (shouldAnimate), pixi-app.ts (createPixiApp factory), PixiWrapper.svelte (canvas mount + context), and index.ts barrel. Fixed .npmrc store-dir path for environment. Build passes clean.

tasks.title: Build geometric particle system with shape generators and presets
tasks.description:
Create the particle system module at `apps/web/src/lib/canvas/particles/`.

1. Create `shapes.ts` — generator functions for geometric shapes: triangle, diamond, hexagon, thin line, dot. Each returns a PixiJS `Graphics` object drawn programmatically with a subtle metallic look (1px lighter offset for chrome sheen effect). Shapes should be small (8-16px).
2. Create `GeometricParticleSystem.ts` — a class that wraps `@pixi/particle-emitter`. API:
   - `burst(x, y, preset)` — one-shot particle burst at a position
   - `startAmbient(preset)` / `stopAmbient()` — continuous particle emission
   - Internal object pool for reuse (cap total active particles at 200)
   - Respects `shouldAnimate()` from reduced-motion module
   - Constructor takes the PixiJS Application stage container
3. Create `presets.ts` — named particle preset configurations:
   - `VOTE_BURST`: 8-12 particles, sharp outward trajectory, 300ms lifetime
   - `AMBIENT_FIELD`: 15-20 slow-drifting hexagons, 0.1 opacity, long lifetime
   - `CELEBRATION`: 30-50 particles upward fountain, gold color
   - `SPARK`: 4-6 tiny fast dots, very short lifetime
   - `SHOCKWAVE_DEBRIS`: 20 radial triangles expanding outward
   - `WINNER_CROWN`: gold upward fountain, similar to CELEBRATION but more concentrated
4. Create `particles/index.ts` barrel export.
acceptance_criteria: shapes.ts exports triangle, diamond, hexagon, line, dot generator functions
GeometricParticleSystem class can burst() and startAmbient()/stopAmbient()
Particle pool caps at 200 active particles
All 6 presets are defined and exported from presets.ts
System respects shouldAnimate() — returns early when reduced motion is preferred
pnpm build completes without errors
tasks.notes: Built custom particle system using PixiJS v8 directly (bypassed @pixi/particle-emitter v5 due to v7/v8 incompatibility). Created shapes.ts (5 shape generators with metallic sheen), presets.ts (6 named presets with ParticlePreset interface), GeometricParticleSystem.ts (sprite-based particle pool, ticker-driven animation, 200 cap), and barrel index.ts. Build passes clean.

tasks.title: Build canvas effects library (shockwave, metallic fill, line draw, glow, grid)
tasks.description: Create the effects module at `apps/web/src/lib/canvas/effects/`. 1. Create `shockwave.ts` — function that creates an expanding ring (PixiJS Graphics circle) that scales from center outward, fading opacity from 0.8 to 0 over 600ms. Self-removes from stage when done. Takes (app, x, y, color?) params. 2. Create `metallic-fill.ts` — function that animates a chrome gradient sweep across a rectangular area left-to-right. Used for histogram bars and tug-of-war fills. Takes (app, rect, color, duration?) params. Returns a Graphics object that can be updated with new dimensions. 3. Create `line-draw.ts` — function that progressively draws a line from point A to point B over a configurable duration. For bracket connectors. Takes (app, startPoint, endPoint, color, duration?) params. 4. Create `glow.ts` — function that creates a focused halo using PixiJS BlurFilter in white or purple. Can be applied to a position or attached to follow an element. Takes (app, x, y, radius, color?) params. 5. Create `grid-overlay.ts` — function that draws a persistent subtle scan-line pattern (horizontal lines at 4px intervals, 0.03 alpha) covering the full viewport. Creates once, resizes on window resize. 6. Create `effects/index.ts` barrel export. Also create `apps/web/src/lib/canvas/rank-insignias.ts` — functions to programmatically render shield/chevron rank badges (bronze, silver, gold, platinum, diamond) using PixiJS Graphics. Each badge is ~24px. Export a function `renderInsignia(rank) => string` that returns a data URL suitable for ``. All effects must respect `shouldAnimate()` from reduced-motion. When reduced motion is active, effects should either skip entirely or render in their final state instantly.
acceptance_criteria: shockwave.ts exports a function that creates an expanding, fading ring effect
metallic-fill.ts exports a function that animates a chrome gradient sweep on a rectangle
line-draw.ts exports a function for progressive line drawing between two points
glow.ts exports a function that creates a blur-filter halo effect
grid-overlay.ts exports a function that draws subtle persistent scan-lines
rank-insignias.ts exports renderInsignia(rank) returning a data URL string
All effects respect reduced-motion preferences
pnpm build completes without errors

tasks.title: Migrate AmbientParticles to canvas (full replacement)
tasks.description:
Rewrite `apps/web/src/lib/components/overlay/AmbientParticles.svelte` to use the PixiJS canvas instead of HTML divs.

Current state: The component renders ~10 div elements with CSS animations for drifting particles.

New implementation:
1. Remove all HTML markup (the drifting div elements) and their CSS.
2. Get the PixiJS app from context via `getContext('pixi-app')`.
3. On mount: create a `GeometricParticleSystem` instance, start it with the `AMBIENT_FIELD` preset. Also initialize the `grid-overlay` scan-line effect.
4. On destroy: call `stopAmbient()` and clean up the grid overlay.
5. The component should render no visible DOM — it's purely a canvas-effect controller.

This is the simplest migration and validates that the entire PixiJS pipeline (PixiWrapper context → particle system) works end-to-end.
acceptance_criteria: AmbientParticles.svelte no longer renders any HTML divs for particles
Component gets pixi-app from Svelte context
Ambient hexagon particles drift on the canvas layer
Grid scan-line overlay is visible at low opacity
Effects stop and clean up when component is destroyed
pnpm build completes without errors

tasks.title: Migrate RoundSplash to hybrid canvas + HTML
tasks.description:
Modify `apps/web/src/lib/components/overlay/RoundSplash.svelte` to use canvas effects for visual flair while keeping text in HTML.

Current state: The component shows 'Round N of M' text with CSS animations including a `.radial-burst` div and `.accent-line` divs.

New implementation:
1. Keep the HTML text ('Round N of M') with its existing `scale-in-overshoot` CSS animation.
2. Remove the `.radial-burst` div and `.accent-line` divs from the markup.
3. Remove the corresponding CSS for those removed elements.
4. Get `pixi-app` from context.
5. On mount: fire `shockwave` effect at viewport center AND `SHOCKWAVE_DEBRIS` particle burst simultaneously. Both auto-clean after ~600ms.
6. Keep the existing `onDone` callback timing (component auto-dismisses after its duration).
acceptance_criteria: RoundSplash.svelte no longer has .radial-burst or .accent-line HTML elements
HTML text 'Round N of M' still renders with CSS animation
Shockwave ring effect fires on canvas at viewport center on mount
SHOCKWAVE_DEBRIS particles burst radially on mount
Effects auto-clean up after completion
onDone callback still fires to dismiss the splash
pnpm build completes without errors

tasks.title: Migrate TugOfWar to hybrid canvas + HTML
tasks.description:
Modify `apps/web/src/lib/components/overlay/TugOfWar.svelte` — the largest migration. Keep interactive/text elements in HTML, move visual fills and effects to canvas.

Current state: Two expanding bar divs with CSS animations, a knot marker, option labels and images, CSS chevrons for momentum.

New implementation:
1. Keep in HTML: Labels (A/B names, percentages, vote count), the knot marker circle (positioned via CSS `left: X%`), option images.
2. Move to Canvas:
   - Bar fills → `metallic-fill` rectangles with chrome sweep animation. Map position from DOM via `getBoundingClientRect()` on the bar container.
   - Rope texture → canvas cross-hatch pattern replacing any SVG overlay.
   - Momentum indicators → geometric particles instead of CSS chevrons.
   - Knot glow → focused `glow` filter on canvas at the knot's DOM position.
3. New Canvas effects:
   - Friction `SPARK` particles at knot position when `isClose` (sides within 10% of each other).
   - `VOTE_BURST` at the side that just received a vote (throttled: max 1 per 100ms).
4. Use `$effect()` watching `percentA`, `percentB`, `totalVotes` to trigger updates.
5. Use a `$effect()` to read bar container `getBoundingClientRect()` and sync canvas positions.
6. Remove replaced CSS animations/keyframes for bar fills, chevrons, etc.
acceptance_criteria: HTML labels, percentages, knot marker, and images still render correctly
Bar fills render as metallic-fill canvas rectangles instead of CSS-animated divs
Knot position has a glow effect on canvas
SPARK particles appear at knot when sides are within 10%
VOTE_BURST fires on the side receiving a vote (throttled to 100ms)
Canvas positions sync with DOM layout via getBoundingClientRect
Removed CSS animations that are now handled by canvas
pnpm build completes without errors

tasks.title: Migrate Histogram to hybrid canvas + HTML
tasks.description:
Modify `apps/web/src/lib/components/overlay/Histogram.svelte` to render bar rectangles on canvas while keeping text in HTML.

Current state: Colored div bars with CSS transitions, vote count numbers above bars (TweenedNumber), labels below bars, total votes pill.

New implementation:
1. Keep in HTML: Vote count numbers above bars (TweenedNumber component), bar labels below, total votes pill.
2. Move to Canvas: Colored bar rectangles → `metallic-fill` Graphics objects with chrome gradient animation.
3. New Canvas effects:
   - `VOTE_BURST` at the top of whichever bar just grew (detect by comparing previous vs current `distribution` values). Throttle to max 1 per 100ms per bar.
   - Leading bar gets a focused purple `glow` filter on canvas.
4. Bar positions: read DOM container layout via `getBoundingClientRect()`, map to canvas coordinates.
5. Use `$effect()` watching `distribution` and `totalVotes` to trigger canvas updates.
6. Remove CSS transitions/animations for the bar fill divs that are now canvas-rendered.
acceptance_criteria: Vote count numbers and labels still render as HTML above/below bars
Bar rectangles render as metallic-fill canvas graphics instead of colored divs
VOTE_BURST particles fire at the top of a bar when its vote count increases
Leading bar has a purple glow effect on canvas
Burst effects are throttled to max 1 per 100ms per bar
Canvas bar positions match the DOM layout
pnpm build completes without errors

tasks.title: Migrate BracketViz to hybrid canvas + HTML
tasks.description:
Modify `apps/web/src/lib/components/overlay/BracketViz.svelte` to render connector lines and celebration effects on canvas.

Current state: Matchup cards in HTML, CSS `::after` pseudo-elements for connector lines between bracket levels, `.sparkle-dot` CSS elements.

New implementation:
1. Keep in HTML: All matchup cards (names, scores, images) — these remain as Svelte/HTML.
2. Move to Canvas: Connector lines between bracket levels → `line-draw` effect with sequential drawing animation (level 0 connectors draw first, then level 1, etc.).
3. Replace: CSS `.sparkle-dot` elements → `CELEBRATION` canvas particles.
4. New: Winner cards get a focused `glow` filter on canvas. The final champion gets a `WINNER_CROWN` particle fountain.
5. Remove CSS `::after` pseudo-elements used for connectors.
6. Read card positions from DOM to calculate connector start/end points for canvas drawing.
7. On bracket completion / winner reveal, fire the celebration effects.
acceptance_criteria: Matchup cards still render correctly as HTML
Connector lines between bracket levels render on canvas with line-draw animation
Connectors draw sequentially — earlier levels before later levels
CSS .sparkle-dot elements are removed and replaced with CELEBRATION particles
Winner cards have a glow effect on canvas
Champion gets WINNER_CROWN particle fountain
CSS ::after connector pseudo-elements are removed
pnpm build completes without errors

tasks.title: Migrate Leaderboard rank badges and GameOverResults celebration
tasks.description: Update Leaderboard to use canvas-rendered rank insignias and GameOverResults to use canvas celebration effects. **Leaderboard.svelte:** 1. Replace emoji medals (trophy/medal emojis) with canvas-rendered rank insignias from `rank-insignias.ts`. 2. Generate insignia images: call `renderInsignia(rank)` for gold/silver/bronze, convert the returned canvas to a data URL, use as `` in the HTML markup. 3. Keep all other HTML (player names, scores, layout) unchanged. **GameOverResults.svelte:** 1. Replace the trophy emoji and any `.sparkle-dot` div elements with a `CELEBRATION` canvas particle burst (50 particles, gold + brand purple colors). 2. Fire the celebration effect on component mount (when game-over phase is entered). 3. Get `pixi-app` from context. 4. Keep all text/layout in HTML. 5. Remove the replaced CSS sparkle animations.
acceptance_criteria: Leaderboard shows rendered shield insignias instead of emoji medals for top 3
Insignias are gold, silver, and bronze colored shields/chevrons
GameOverResults fires a CELEBRATION particle burst on mount
Trophy emoji and .sparkle-dot elements are removed from GameOverResults
Celebration uses gold and brand purple particle colors
All HTML text and layout remains intact in both components

tasks.title: Integrate PixiWrapper into overlay page and clean up replaced CSS
tasks.description:
Wire everything together by mounting the PixiWrapper in the overlay page and removing CSS that has been replaced by canvas effects.

**Modify `apps/web/src/routes/overlay/[sessionId]/+page.svelte`:**
1. Import `PixiWrapper` from `$lib/canvas`.
2. Wrap the entire `.overlay-container` content with `<PixiWrapper>`. The canvas renders as the first element at z-index 0 with `pointer-events: none`. All Svelte HTML layers sit on top at higher z-index.
3. AmbientParticles already renders conditionally during `live` + `round-results` phases — this logic stays as-is.
4. Verify that the PixiWrapper canvas has transparent background so OBS browser source shows through.

**Clean up `apps/web/src/app.css`:**
1. Remove any CSS `@keyframes` that were used by the old HTML-based particle divs, sparkle dots, radial burst, accent lines, etc. — anything now handled by canvas.
2. Keep all CSS that is still in use (transitions for HTML text, Tailwind utilities, etc.).

**Final verification:**
1. Run `pnpm build` to ensure no TypeScript or build errors.
2. Run `pnpm run check` (lint + typecheck) to ensure code quality.
3. Verify all overlay components import from the canvas module correctly.
4. Confirm no orphaned CSS keyframes or unused imports remain.
acceptance_criteria: PixiWrapper wraps the overlay page content
Canvas element renders at z-index 0 with pointer-events: none and transparent background
All HTML content layers render above the canvas
Replaced CSS keyframes and animations are removed from app.css
No orphaned imports or unused CSS remain
pnpm build completes without errors
pnpm run check passes (lint + typecheck)

tasks.title: Add rank tier constants, streak multiplier logic, and round-XP types to shared-types
tasks.description:
Add the foundational XP system constants, rank tier mapping, streak multiplier logic, and new socket event types to `packages/shared-types/src/gamification.ts` and `packages/shared-types/src/socket-events.ts`.

**1. Named Rank Tiers** — Add to `packages/shared-types/src/gamification.ts` after existing enums:
- `RankTier` enum: BRONZE, SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM, DIAMOND
- `RANK_TIER_THRESHOLDS: Record<RankTier, number>` mapping: BRONZE=0, SILVER=500, GOLD=1500, PLATINUM=3500, DIAMOND=7000
- `computeRankTier(totalXp: number): RankTier` — returns highest rank whose threshold the player meets
- `xpToNextRank(totalXp: number): { current: RankTier; next: RankTier | null; xpNeeded: number }` — progress toward next rank

**2. Streak Multiplier** — Add constants and utility:
- `STREAK_MULTIPLIERS = { THRESHOLD_2X: 3, THRESHOLD_3X: 5, MULTIPLIER_2X: 2, MULTIPLIER_3X: 3 } as const`
- `getStreakMultiplier(consecutiveCorrect: number): number` — returns 1 for <3, 2 for 3-4, 3 for >=5

**3. Round-end XP broadcast types** — Add new interfaces:
- `RoundXpSummary`: `{ round: number; playerXp: Record<string, { roundXp: number; totalSessionXp: number; streak: number; multiplier: number }> }`
- `RankUpEventData`: `{ previousRank: RankTier; newRank: RankTier; totalXp: number }`
- `XpTickEventData`: `{ amount: number; reason: XpReason; multiplier: number }`

**4. Extend GamificationEvent** — Update the `type` union to include `'rank_up' | 'xp_tick'` and add `RankUpEventData | XpTickEventData` to the `data` union.

**5. Add socket event** — In `packages/shared-types/src/socket-events.ts`, add to `ServerToClientEvents`: `'gamification:round-xp': (data: RoundXpSummary) => void;`

**6. Update PlayerProfileSummary** — Add `rankTier: RankTier` field.

Run `pnpm build` from workspace root to verify shared-types compiles and downstream packages resolve the new exports.
acceptance_criteria: RankTier enum with BRONZE/SILVER/GOLD/PLATINUM/DIAMOND is exported
RANK_TIER_THRESHOLDS maps each rank to correct XP threshold (0/500/1500/3500/7000)
computeRankTier(totalXp) returns the correct rank for given XP values
xpToNextRank returns current rank, next rank, and XP needed
STREAK_MULTIPLIERS constants and getStreakMultiplier function are exported
getStreakMultiplier returns 1 for <3, 2 for 3-4, 3 for >=5
RoundXpSummary, RankUpEventData, XpTickEventData interfaces are exported
GamificationEvent type union includes rank_up and xp_tick
ServerToClientEvents includes gamification:round-xp
PlayerProfileSummary includes rankTier field
pnpm build completes without errors

tasks.title: Add streak multiplier logic to GamificationService
tasks.description:
Modify `apps/server/src/services/GamificationService.ts` to apply streak multipliers (2x at streak 3, 3x at streak 5) when recording correct answers.

**Changes to `recordCorrectAnswer` method:**
1. Import `getStreakMultiplier` from `@twitch-hub/shared-types`.
2. After incrementing the streak counter in Redis (`const newStreak = await redis.incr(sKey)`), compute the multiplier: `const multiplier = getStreakMultiplier(newStreak);`
3. Replace the flat `STREAK_BONUS_PER_STEP` with a multiplied correct-answer XP:
   - Base correct answer XP is already awarded (`XP_AWARDS.CORRECT_ANSWER`).
   - If `multiplier > 1`, award additional bonus XP: `const bonusXp = XP_AWARDS.CORRECT_ANSWER * (multiplier - 1);`
   - Replace the existing streak bonus increment with: `await redis.hincrby(key, 'STREAK_BONUS', bonusXp);` and matching leaderboard zincrby.
   - Result: streak 3 yields 5 + 5 = 10 XP (2x), streak 5 yields 5 + 10 = 15 XP (3x).
4. Update the streak milestone broadcast to include the multiplier value in the event data.
5. Update `StreakEventData` in shared-types to include optional `multiplier?: number` field if not already present.

Verify that `finalizeSession` still correctly aggregates the `STREAK_BONUS` field from Redis — the key name hasn't changed, just the amounts.
acceptance_criteria: getStreakMultiplier is imported and used in recordCorrectAnswer
At streak 3-4, STREAK_BONUS awarded is XP_AWARDS.CORRECT_ANSWER * 1 (the extra 1x)
At streak >=5, STREAK_BONUS awarded is XP_AWARDS.CORRECT_ANSWER * 2 (the extra 2x)
Streak milestone broadcast includes multiplier in event data
StreakEventData interface includes optional multiplier field
finalizeSession correctly sums up variable STREAK_BONUS amounts from Redis
pnpm build completes without errors

tasks.title: Add round-end XP broadcast to gameHandler and game store
tasks.description:
Currently, XP summaries are only emitted at game END. Add per-round XP data emission so the overlay can show floating +XP numbers.

**1. Add `getRoundXpSummary` method to GamificationService** (`apps/server/src/services/GamificationService.ts`):
- Signature: `async getRoundXpSummary(sessionId: string, round: number, participantIds: string[]): Promise<RoundXpSummary>`
- For each participant: read their XP hash from Redis, compute total session XP, diff against a stored previous total (use Redis key `session:{sessionId}:prevxp:{playerId}`), read streak count, compute multiplier.
- Return `RoundXpSummary` with per-player `{ roundXp, totalSessionXp, streak, multiplier }` for players whose roundXp > 0.

**2. Modify `game:next-round` handler** in `apps/server/src/socket/handlers/gameHandler.ts`:
- After `broadcastToSession(io, sessionId, 'game:round-end', results)`, add:
  ```
  const roundXpSummary = await gamificationService.getRoundXpSummary(sessionId, results.round, [...engine.getParticipantIds()]);
  broadcastToSession(io, sessionId, 'gamification:round-xp', roundXpSummary);
  ```
- Wrap in try/catch with error logging.

**3. Handle auto-timer path** in `apps/server/src/engine/GameEngine.ts`:
- Add callback: `private onRoundXpCallback?: (sessionId: string, round: number) => Promise<void>;` with setter method.
- In `onRoundTimerExpired`, after leaderboard broadcast, call `this.onRoundXpCallback?.(this.sessionId, results.round)`.
- Wire this callback in GameRegistry's `initSession` to call `gamificationService.getRoundXpSummary` and broadcast.

**4. Update game store** (`apps/web/src/lib/stores/game.svelte.ts`):
- Add `roundXpSummary: RoundXpSummary | null` to state (default null).
- Add getter for roundXpSummary.
- Listen for `'gamification:round-xp'` event, set `state.roundXpSummary = data`.
- Reset `roundXpSummary` to null on `game:round-start`.
acceptance_criteria: GamificationService has getRoundXpSummary method returning per-player round XP deltas
game:next-round handler broadcasts gamification:round-xp after round-end
onRoundTimerExpired in GameEngine also triggers round XP broadcast via callback
GameRegistry wires the round XP callback during initSession
game.svelte.ts stores roundXpSummary and resets it on round-start
RoundXpSummary type from shared-types is used consistently
pnpm build completes without errors

tasks.title: Add rank-up detection to GamificationService finalizeSession
tasks.description:
Modify `apps/server/src/services/GamificationService.ts` to detect when a player crosses a rank tier threshold during session finalization, and broadcast a `rank_up` gamification event.

**Changes to `finalizeSession` method** (inside the transaction loop after profile update):

1. Import `computeRankTier, RankTier` from `@twitch-hub/shared-types`.
2. Before the `tx.playerProfile.update` that increments `totalXp`, read the player's current `totalXp` to determine their pre-update rank:
   ```ts
   const preUpdateProfile = await tx.playerProfile.findUnique({ where: { id: profileId }, select: { totalXp: true } });
   const previousRank = computeRankTier(preUpdateProfile?.totalXp ?? 0);
   ```
3. After the profile update (which increments totalXp), compute the new rank:
   ```ts
   const newRank = computeRankTier(updatedProfile.totalXp);
   ```
4. If `newRank !== previousRank`, broadcast a `rank_up` event:
   ```ts
   this.broadcastCallback?.(sessionId, 'gamification:event', {
     type: 'rank_up',
     playerId,
     displayName: playerId,
     data: { previousRank, newRank, totalXp: updatedProfile.totalXp },
   } satisfies GamificationEvent);
   ```
5. Also update the existing `level_up` broadcast to include rank info in the event data if the level-up also triggers a rank change.
acceptance_criteria: Pre-update rank is computed from current totalXp before incrementing
Post-update rank is computed after totalXp increment
rank_up GamificationEvent is broadcast when rank changes (e.g., BRONZE -> SILVER)
rank_up event includes previousRank, newRank, and totalXp
No rank_up event fires if rank stays the same
GamificationEvent type annotation satisfies the updated union including rank_up
pnpm build completes without errors

tasks.title: Add rank tier to profile API responses and create global leaderboard endpoint
tasks.description:
Update the gamification REST API to include rank tier information in profile responses, and add a new global XP leaderboard endpoint.

**1. Update profile responses** in `apps/server/src/routes/gamification.ts`:
- Import `computeRankTier` from `@twitch-hub/shared-types`.
- In both `/profile` and `/profile/:twitchLogin` response objects, add: `rankTier: computeRankTier(profile.totalXp)`
- Add this field alongside the existing `totalXp`, `level`, etc. fields.

**2. Create global leaderboard endpoint** — Add a new route in the same `gamificationPlugin`:
- `GET /leaderboard` with query params `limit` (default 50, max 100) and `offset` (default 0).
- Query `prisma.playerProfile.findMany` ordered by `totalXp` desc, with limit/offset, including user relation for displayName, profileImageUrl, twitchLogin.
- Also query `prisma.playerProfile.count()` for total.
- Return: `{ entries: [{ rank, twitchLogin, displayName, profileImageUrl, totalXp, level, rankTier }], total, limit, offset }`
- Each entry's `rankTier` computed via `computeRankTier(p.totalXp)`.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/gamification/profile response includes rankTier field
GET /api/gamification/profile/:twitchLogin response includes rankTier field
GET /api/gamification/leaderboard returns paginated global XP leaderboard
Leaderboard entries include rank, twitchLogin, displayName, profileImageUrl, totalXp, level, rankTier
Leaderboard supports limit (default 50, max 100) and offset query params
Leaderboard returns total count for pagination UI
pnpm build completes without errors

tasks.title: Create RankBadge component and update profile page with rank display
tasks.description:
Create a RankBadge Svelte component and update the player profile page to show the player's rank tier.

**1. Create `apps/web/src/lib/components/gamification/RankBadge.svelte`:**
- Props: `{ tier: RankTier; size?: 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' }`
- Color mapping per tier:
  - BRONZE: from-amber-700 to-amber-500, border-amber-600
  - SILVER: from-gray-400 to-gray-200, border-gray-300
  - GOLD: from-yellow-500 to-yellow-300, border-yellow-400
  - PLATINUM: from-cyan-400 to-blue-300, border-cyan-300
  - DIAMOND: from-cyan-300 to-teal-200, border-cyan-200 with shimmer animation
- Rendering: Pill/badge shape with metallic gradient background, rank name in bold uppercase, thin tier-colored border.
- Size variants: sm (text-xs, px-2 py-0.5), md (text-sm, px-3 py-1), lg (text-base, px-4 py-1.5).
- Diamond rank: `background-size: 200% auto; animation: shimmer 3s linear infinite`.

**2. Update `apps/web/src/routes/u/[twitchLogin]/+page.svelte`:**
- Import `RankBadge` and `RankTier` from shared-types.
- In the header section after the display name, add: `{#if profile.rankTier}<RankBadge tier={profile.rankTier} size="md" />{/if}`

**3. Update `apps/web/src/lib/components/gamification/XpBar.svelte`:**
- Add optional `rankTier` prop.
- Display rank name next to the level badge when provided.
acceptance_criteria: RankBadge.svelte renders a pill badge with the rank name in metallic gradient styling
All 5 rank tiers have distinct color schemes
Diamond rank has a shimmer animation
Size variants sm/md/lg produce correctly sized badges
Profile page shows the rank badge next to the player's display name
XpBar optionally shows rank tier name alongside level
pnpm build completes without errors

tasks.title: Create global XP leaderboard page
tasks.description:
Create a new SvelteKit route at `apps/web/src/routes/leaderboard/` that displays a paginated global XP leaderboard.

**1. Create `apps/web/src/routes/leaderboard/+page.svelte`:**
- Page title: 'Global Leaderboard'
- Fetches from `GET /api/gamification/leaderboard` with limit/offset query params.
- Leaderboard table columns: Rank, Player (avatar + display name), Rank Tier (RankBadge component), Level, Total XP.
- Top 3 players get visually highlighted rows (gold/silver/bronze backgrounds).
- Each player row links to their profile: `/u/{twitchLogin}`.
- Pagination: Previous/Next buttons, showing 'Page X of Y'. 50 entries per page.
- Loading state while data fetches.
- Use Svelte 5 runes: `$state` for entries/loading/page, `$derived` for totalPages.
- Use existing Tailwind design system (bg-surface-primary, text-text-primary, etc.) consistent with the profile page.

**2. Add `<svelte:head><title>Leaderboard - Twitch Hub</title></svelte:head>` for SEO.**

**3. Add navigation link** — Find the main app navigation component and add a 'Leaderboard' link pointing to `/leaderboard`.
acceptance_criteria: Route /leaderboard renders a global XP leaderboard page
Page fetches from /api/gamification/leaderboard endpoint
Table shows rank, player avatar+name, rank badge, level, and total XP
Top 3 players have visually highlighted rows
Player rows link to /u/{twitchLogin} profile pages
Pagination with Previous/Next works correctly
Loading state shown while data fetches
Navigation includes a Leaderboard link
pnpm build completes without errors

tasks.title: Add XP summary and rank badges to GameOverResults overlay component
tasks.description:
Extend `apps/web/src/lib/components/overlay/GameOverResults.svelte` to show per-player XP earned during the session and a personal XP breakdown. **1. Update GameOverResults.svelte:** - Add new optional props: `sessionSummary: SessionXpSummary | null` and `leaderboard: LeaderboardEntry[]`. - After the existing game-type-specific results block, add two new sections with stagger-reveal animations: **Session XP Leaderboard** (animation-delay: 1200ms): ```svelte {#if leaderboard.length > 0}

Session XP Leaderboard

({ userId: e.playerId, score: e.xp }))} title="XP Earned" />
{/if} ``` **Personal XP Breakdown** (animation-delay: 1600ms): ```svelte {#if sessionSummary}

Your XP Breakdown

{#each Object.entries(sessionSummary.breakdown) as [reason, xp]}
{formatXpReason(reason)}+{xp}
{/each}
Total: +{sessionSummary.total} XP
{/if} ``` - Add `formatXpReason(reason: string): string` helper mapping XpReason values to labels: PARTICIPATION→'Participation', CORRECT_ANSWER→'Correct Answers', STREAK_BONUS→'Streak Multiplier Bonus', etc. **2. Update overlay page** (`apps/web/src/routes/overlay/[sessionId]/+page.svelte`): - Pass `sessionSummary={gameStore.sessionSummary}` and `leaderboard={gameStore.leaderboard}` to GameOverResults. **3. Update play page** if it also uses GameOverResults — pass the same props.
acceptance_criteria: GameOverResults shows session XP leaderboard when leaderboard data is available
GameOverResults shows personal XP breakdown when sessionSummary is available
XP breakdown displays human-readable labels for each XP reason
XP breakdown shows total with a separator line
Stagger-reveal animation delays are sequential (1200ms, 1600ms)
Overlay page passes sessionSummary and leaderboard props to GameOverResults
pnpm build completes without errors

tasks.title: Build canvas floating +XP numbers and XP progress bar effects
tasks.description:
Create canvas XP animation effects that render on the overlay during gameplay. These require the PixiJS infrastructure from tasks 1-3.

**1. Create `apps/web/src/lib/canvas/effects/floating-xp.ts`:**
- Export: `function spawnFloatingXp(app: Application, x: number, y: number, amount: number, options?: { color?: number; fontSize?: number; duration?: number }): void`
- Creates a PixiJS `Text` object with `+{amount} XP` label.
- Font: bold, 18px default (configurable), white with drop shadow.
- Animates upward ~60px over 1200ms with easeOutQuart from theme.
- Fades opacity from 1 to 0 over the same duration.
- If streak multiplier > 1, caller passes gold color (0xfbbf24) and 22px font.
- Self-removes from stage when animation completes.
- Respects `shouldAnimate()` — skips if reduced motion preferred.

**2. Create `apps/web/src/lib/canvas/effects/xp-progress-bar.ts`:**
- Export: `class CanvasXpProgressBar` with:
  - `constructor(app: Application, y: number, width: number, height: number)`
  - `update(progress: number, rankColor: number): void` — progress 0-1
  - `animateFill(from: number, to: number, duration?: number): void` — smooth tween
  - `destroy(): void`
- Renders a horizontal bar with dark background track and metallic gradient fill.
- Uses the `metallic-fill` effect from task 3 for chrome sweep.
- Fill color matches the player's current rank tier.
- `animateFill` smoothly tweens fill width from `from` to `to` over `duration` ms.
- On rank change, flash the bar with a `glow` effect.

**3. Update barrel exports** (`apps/web/src/lib/canvas/effects/index.ts` and `apps/web/src/lib/canvas/index.ts`) to include both new effects.
acceptance_criteria: spawnFloatingXp creates an animated +XP text that drifts up and fades out
Floating XP text uses gold color and larger font when multiplier > 1
Floating XP respects reduced motion preferences
CanvasXpProgressBar renders a metallic gradient fill bar
animateFill smoothly tweens the progress bar width
Progress bar uses rank-tier-appropriate colors
Both effects self-clean from the PixiJS stage when done/destroyed
Effects are exported from the canvas barrel module
pnpm build completes without errors

tasks.title: Build canvas level-up/rank-up celebrations and wire XP effects into overlay
tasks.description:
Create the remaining canvas celebration effects and wire all XP-related canvas effects into the overlay page by consuming gamification events from the game store.

**1. Create `apps/web/src/lib/canvas/effects/level-up-effect.ts`:**
- Export: `function playLevelUpEffect(app: Application, x: number, y: number): void`
- Sequence: shockwave at (x, y) with brand purple → 200ms delay → CELEBRATION particle burst (30 particles, brand purple + white).
- Uses existing `shockwave.ts` from task 3.

**2. Create `apps/web/src/lib/canvas/effects/rank-up-effect.ts`:**
- Export: `function playRankUpEffect(app: Application, newRank: RankTier, centerX: number, centerY: number): void`
- Sequence:
  1. Screen-center shockwave with the new rank's color
  2. 300ms later: SHOCKWAVE_DEBRIS particles in rank color
  3. 500ms later: Rank insignia (from `rank-insignias.ts` task 3) as PixiJS Sprite at center, scale from 0→1.2→1.0 (overshoot bounce), hold 2 seconds, then fade out
  4. Gold CELEBRATION particles during insignia reveal

**3. Create `apps/web/src/lib/components/overlay/XpEffectsController.svelte`:**
- Renderless Svelte 5 component (no visible DOM).
- Gets `pixi-app` from context.
- Watches `gameStore.roundXpSummary` via `$effect()`: when new summary arrives, spawn floating +XP for each player (staggered 50ms apart). Position: random x across viewport, y at bottom third.
- Watches `gameStore.gamificationQueue` via `$effect()`: consume `level_up` events → `playLevelUpEffect`, consume `rank_up` events → `playRankUpEffect`. Dequeue after triggering.
- All effects respect reduced motion.

**4. Mount in overlay page** (`apps/web/src/routes/overlay/[sessionId]/+page.svelte`):
- Import and add `<XpEffectsController />` inside the `<PixiWrapper>` (from task 10).
- Active during `live`, `round-results`, and `game-over` phases.

**5. Update barrel exports** to include new effects.
acceptance_criteria: Level-up triggers a shockwave + celebration particle burst on the canvas
Rank-up triggers shockwave + debris + animated insignia reveal with rank-colored particles
Rank insignia scales in with overshoot bounce and fades out after 2 seconds
Floating +XP numbers appear on canvas when round XP summary arrives
XP numbers use gold color and larger font when streak multiplier > 1
XpEffectsController correctly consumes and dequeues gamification events
XpEffectsController is mounted in the overlay page within PixiWrapper
Effects only trigger during live/round-results/game-over phases
All canvas effects respect reduced motion preferences
pnpm build completes without errors
